prs.title: [Feat] dflash support for ROCm
prs.author: hangy-amd
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

There's already dflash support for nvidia https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/36847. This PR is for dflash support for ROCm

## Benchmark

Model: `Qwen/Qwen3-8B`
Drafter: `z-lab/Qwen3-8B-DFlash-b16`
Num prompts per run: `8`
Max new tokens per request: `128`
Concurrencies: `[32, 16, 8, 1]`
Datasets (official dflash benchmark): `['gsm8k', 'math500', 'humaneval', 'mbpp', 'mt-bench']`
GPU: `AMD MI300`

I measured the following speedups using the DFlash methodology (https://github.com/z-lab/dflash)

### gsm8k

| conc | baseline tok/s | dflash tok/s | speedup |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 32 | 382.88 | 938.71 | 2.452x |
| 16 | 275.44 | 1064.00 | 3.863x |
| 8 | 395.10 | 1030.10 | 2.607x |
| 1 | 69.73 | 145.81 | 2.091x |

### math500

| conc | baseline tok/s | dflash tok/s | speedup |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 32 | 401.03 | 1079.76 | 2.692x |
| 16 | 366.08 | 997.11 | 2.724x |
| 8 | 394.34 | 1460.42 | 3.703x |
| 1 | 76.43 | 224.46 | 2.937x |

### humaneval

| conc | baseline tok/s | dflash tok/s | speedup |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 32 | 405.17 | 922.34 | 2.276x |
| 16 | 385.34 | 868.47 | 2.254x |
| 8 | 398.33 | 1253.89 | 3.148x |
| 1 | 76.06 | 200.50 | 2.636x |

### mbpp

| conc | baseline tok/s | dflash tok/s | speedup |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 32 | 398.50 | 686.99 | 1.724x |
| 16 | 405.31 | 537.49 | 1.326x |
| 8 | 407.93 | 933.81 | 2.289x |
| 1 | 77.89 | 206.02 | 2.645x |

### mt-bench

| conc | baseline tok/s | dflash tok/s | speedup |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 32 | 383.87 | 1172.04 | 3.053x |
| 16 | 375.00 | 514.22 | 1.371x |
| 8 | 387.94 | 423.75 | 1.092x |
| 1 | 75.99 | 82.47 | 1.085x |

## Accuracy

I tested GSM8K accuracy with command:
```bash
/opt/conda/envs/quark-env/bin/python tests/evals/gsm8k/gsm8k_eval.py \
  --host <host> \
  --port <port> \
  --num-questions 200 \
  --num-shots 5 \
  --max-tokens 256 \
  --temperature 0.0
```
The results are:
||baseline|dflash|
|---|---|---|

prs.title: [Bugfix] Fix scaled_mm output narrowing for 3D input tensors
prs.author: nemanjaudovic
prs.body_head:
When the input activation x is 3D (e.g. [batch, seq, hidden_in]), the base class flattens it to 2D before calling apply_scaled_mm, but passes the original 3D shape as
  output_shape. The narrowing step then used output_shape[0] (batch only) instead of the full number of tokens (which for 3D inputs is output_shape[0] * output_shape[1], or in 
  general math.prod(output_shape[:-1])). Fix by using math.prod(output_shape[:-1]) in all three pytorch kernel variants so the correct number of rows is used regardless of    
  input rank.                                                                                                                                                                   
                  
  Co-authored-by: Claude
                                                                                                                                                                                
  Purpose                                                                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                                
  Fix incorrect output narrowing in pytorch FP8 scaled_mm kernels when input activations are 3D tensors. When initializing the Phi model with:                                  
```python                  
  from vllm import LLM                                                                                                                                                          
  llm = LLM(                                                                                                                                                                    
      model='nm-testing/Phi-3.5-vision-instruct-FP8-dynamic',

prs.title: Rocm/fused short seq attention
prs.author: MohamedSayedFathy
prs.body_head:
<!-- markdownlint-disable -->

## Purpose

Optimize PagedAttention v2 decode performance on ROCm for short sequences (≤256 tokens) by eliminating the reduce kernel when only a single partition is needed.

When `max_num_partitions == 1`, the QKV kernel writes directly to `final_out` instead of `tmp_out`, skipping:
- The `tmp_out` intermediate buffer write (~8 KB per seq/layer)
- The `tmp_out` read-back in the reduce kernel (~8 KB per seq/layer)
- The `exp_sums`/`max_logits` metadata writes (~256 bytes per seq/layer)
- The reduce kernel launch entirely (~10μs overhead)

This saves ~16.5 KB of HBM traffic per sequence per layer and eliminates one kernel launch.

### Key design choices:
- Uses `if constexpr (SINGLE_PARTITION)` for zero-cost abstraction — dead code eliminated at compile time
- Enabled by default, togglable via `VLLM_ROCM_FUSED_SHORT_SEQ=0` for A/B benchmarking
- Applied to both mfma4 (GQA ≤ 4) and mfma16 (GQA 5-16) kernels on MI300X
- No changes to the multi-partition path — long sequences are unaffected

## Test Plan

Verified on AMD MI300X by running `benchmark_paged_attention.py` with both `VLLM_ROCM_FUSED_SHORT_SEQ=1` (optimized) and `VLLM_ROCM_FUSED_SHORT_SEQ=0` (baseline). Both paths produce correct results with matching outputs.

```bash
# Optimized path (default)
python benchmarks/kernels/benchmark_paged_attention.py \
  --version v2 --custom-paged-attn --seq-len 128 --batch-size 8

# Baseline path (disabled)
VLLM_ROCM_FUSED_SHORT_SEQ=0 python benchmarks/kernels/benchmark_paged_attention.py \
  --version v2 --custom-paged-attn --seq-len 128 --batch-size 8

prs.title: [WIP] Add TRITON_MLA_SPARSE backend for SM80 sparse MLA support
prs.author: haosdent
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Closes #38006. Enables sparse MLA models (GLM-5, DeepSeek-V3.2) on SM80 (A100/A800) and SM121 (GB10/DGX Spark), where DeepGEMM / FlashMLA-Sparse / FlashInfer-MLA-Sparse are unavailable.

Three changes:

1. **Guard fix.** `has_deep_gemm()` (package check) → `is_deep_gemm_supported()` (SM90+ arch check) in `sparse_attn_indexer.py` and `indexer.py`. Prevents DeepGEMM SM90+ kernels from being invoked on SM80/SM121 when the Python package happens to be installed.

2. **Triton indexer MQA logits.** `mqa_logits_triton.py` reproduces DeepGEMM's `fp8_mqa_logits` / `fp8_paged_mqa_logits` (bf16 tensor-core matmul with fp32 accumulator + scale, ReLU + per-head weighting, sum across heads). PyTorch reference stays in `mqa_logits_fallback.py`. On GB10, `fp8_paged_mqa_logits` at ctx=2048 goes from 6.4 ms (torch loop) → 0.03 ms (~250×), saving ~380 ms/token across 60 sparse layers.

3. **New `TRITON_MLA_SPARSE` backend** (`triton_mla_sparse.py` → `triton_sparse_mla_kernel.py`). Single pure-Triton kernel with split-KV for low-batch decode (`num_kv_splits > 1` when the single-pass grid under-fills the device) and a single-pass fast path otherwise. Stage 2 merges per-split `(out, lse)` tiles via online-softmax rescaling (pattern from `triton_decode_attention.py`). `@triton.autotune` over `(num_warps, num_stages)` × `(index_topk, NUM_KV_SPLITS)`; warmed in `TritonMLASparseImpl.__init__` so the first user request does not pay the sweep cost. Registered in `AttentionBackendEnum` and added to the non-SM100 MLA priority list.

**Kernel benchmark on NVIDIA A30 (SM80, 56 SMs):**

| shape | XPU kernel | this PR | speedup |
|---|---|---|---|
| nt=1, topk=2048, H=128 (decode) | 0.297 ms | 0.094 ms | **3.15×** |
| nt=1, topk=4096, H=128 | 0.580 ms | 0.167 ms | 3.46× |
| nt=1, topk=4096, H=16 | 0.582 ms | 0.081 ms | 7.17× |
| nt=128, topk=2048, H=128 (prefill) | 4.912 ms | 4.872 ms | 1.01× |

Decode (nt=1) across all (topk, H): median **4.30×**, best 7.17×. Non-decode (nt ≥ 8): autotu

prs.title: [ROCm] Allow Triton MXFP4 MoE support checks on gfx11xx
prs.author: wangrui6
prs.body_head:
Add a shared helper for Triton MXFP4 device support checks and use it in the GPT-OSS Triton MXFP4 experts support paths.

This keeps the CUDA < 11.0 guard unchanged, while allowing ROCm gfx11xx devices (for example gfx1151 / RDNA3.5) to pass the support check.

Also add unit coverage for the platform-specific capability ceilings.

Tested with:
- .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/kernels/moe/test_mxfp4_support.py -v

AI assistance was used to prepare this change, and I reviewed the final diff.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex




## Purpose
This PR fixes the Triton MXFP4 MoE support check for ROCm gfx11xx devices such as gfx1151 / RDNA3.5.
Changes:
- add a shared helper for Triton MXFP4 device support checks
- keep the existing CUDA `< 11.0` guard unchanged
- allow ROCm gfx11xx devices through the Triton MXFP4 support path
- add unit coverage for the platform-specific capability ceilings

### Why this is not duplicate work

I checked issue #40301 and searched for open PRs referencing `#40301` and related terms before opening this PR. I did not find an open PR covering this specific ROCm gfx11xx support-check fix.

## Test Plan
Ran:
- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/kernels/moe/test_mxfp4_support.py -v`

## Test Result
Result:
- `3 passed`

## AI assistance

This is an AI-assisted contribution. I reviewed the final diff and verified the test command above before submitting.
